skill.name: pollinations-ai
skill.description: Generate and save images using Pollinations.ai's free, open-source API. No signup required. Supports URL-based generation, custom parameters (width, height, model, seed), and automatic file saving. Perfect for quick prototypes, marketing assets, and creative workflows.
skill.summary: Generate and save images using Pollinations.ai's free, open-source API. No signup required. Supports...
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Security audit confirms this is a legitimate image generation skill. Static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code delimiters (not Ruby execution), network calls target the official Pollinations.ai API (not malicious endpoints), file writes save generated images (expected behavior), and path traversal references are markdown links in documentation. No actual security risks identified.

medium_findings.title: Documentation References Misidentified as Path Traversal
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected '..' sequences in markdown links at lines 397-399. These are documentation cross-references to related skills, not code vulnerabilities.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines contain markdown relative links: ../image-generation/ - clearly documentation references, not path traversal in executable code

low_findings.title: Code Block Delimiters Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Scanner detected 59 instances of backticks as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown code block fences (```), not actual code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backticks appear in markdown code block delimiters - standard documentation formatting, not executable code

low_findings.title: API Endpoints Flagged as Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged Pollinations.ai API URLs as 'Hardcoded URL'. These are the legitimate service endpoints required for the skill's core functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to image.pollinations.ai - the official public API for this image generation service, not external malicious sites

low_findings.title: HTTP Library Usage Flagged
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'requests.get()' as network access. This is the legitimate method to call the Pollinations.ai API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Python requests library used to fetch images from legitimate API - expected behavior for image generation skill

low_findings.title: File Write Operations Flagged
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged file write operations at lines 115 and 173. These save generated images to disk - core functionality of the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File writes save API response (generated images) to user-specified paths - expected behavior for image generation

content.user_title: Generate Free AI Images via Pollinations API
content.value_statement: Need images but want to avoid paid APIs? This skill generates images through Pollinations.ai's free, open API with no signup required.
actual_capabilities: Generate images via simple URL-based API calls to Pollinations.ai
Save generated images to local files with Python automation
Create multiple variations using seed values for reproducibility
Support multiple AI models: flux, turbo, and stable-diffusion
Batch generate image variations for testing and selection
limitations: Depends on external Pollinations.ai service availability
Image quality varies based on prompt specificity
No advanced editing or inpainting capabilities

use_cases.title: Quick Prototyping
use_cases.description: Generate placeholder images instantly for website mockups, app designs, or presentation slides without waiting for design resources.
use_cases.target_user: Developers and product managers needing rapid visual assets

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creation
use_cases.description: Create hero images, social media banners, and promotional graphics without paid subscriptions or stock photo licenses.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and small business owners

use_cases.title: Creative Exploration
use_cases.description: Rapidly test different visual styles, color schemes, and compositions to find the right direction before committing to final designs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe your subject] using the Pollinations.ai skill. Save it as [filename.jpg].
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple single image generation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Styled Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [style] image of [subject] with [lighting] lighting and [mood] mood. Use dimensions [width]x[height]. Save as [filename].
prompt_templates.scenario: Custom styled image with specific dimensions

prompt_templates.title: Batch Variation Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate 4 variations of [describe the image concept] using seeds [100, 200, 300, 400]. Use the flux model at 1920x1080. Save each as variant_[seed].jpg.

prompt_templates.title: Brand-Aligned Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image with brand colors [color 1] and [color 2]. Style: [modern/minimal/professional]. Dimensions: [width]x[height] for [platform]. Include [any specific elements]. Save with metadata to generation_log.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating consistent brand assets with documentation

output_examples.input: Generate a hero image for a tech startup website
output_examples.output: A 1920x1080 hero image with modern gradient background in blue and purple tones, abstract geometric shapes, space for text on left side. Professional, clean aesthetic suitable for SaaS landing page.

output_examples.input: Create a LinkedIn banner with brand colors
output_examples.output: 1584x396 LinkedIn banner using brand colors #2563EB and #8B5CF6, abstract design with geometric shapes, text-safe zone on left for name/headline placement.
Four 1024x1024 square thumbnails showing futuristic dashboard UI concepts with dark theme and subtle glow effects. Each uses different seed value for subtle style variations.

best_practices: Use specific, detailed prompts with style, lighting, mood, and quality modifiers for best results
Set dimensions early to match target platform requirements and avoid unwanted cropping
Use seed values to reproduce successful images and iterate on variations
anti_patterns: Using vague prompts like 'make it look good' - results in generic, unmemorable images
Ignoring aspect ratio requirements - leads to cropping or distortion on target platforms
Not saving metadata - makes it impossible to reproduce or refine successful generations

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use Pollinations.ai?
faq.answer: No. Pollinations.ai is completely free and requires no signup or API key. Just construct the URL with your prompt and parameters.

faq.question: What image models are available?
faq.answer: Three models: flux (highest quality), turbo (fastest for iterations), and stable-diffusion (balanced quality and speed).

faq.question: How do I get consistent results?
faq.answer: Use the seed parameter. Same prompt + same seed = same image every time. This is essential for reproducibility.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes. Pollinations.ai images are open source. Check their license for specific commercial usage terms.

faq.question: Why do I need URL encoding?
faq.answer: Special characters in prompts (spaces, symbols) must be encoded. Use urllib.parse.quote() in Python to properly encode your prompt.

faq.question: What dimensions should I use?
faq.answer: Depends on your platform: Instagram posts are 1:1 (1080x1080), LinkedIn banners are 1584x396, YouTube thumbnails are 16:9 (1280x720).
